story.title: Captain Redbeard's Secret
story.text: Last summer, a brave boy named Tom visited his grandfather, a retired sailor. Grandfather showed Tom an old wooden box. Inside, there was a faded map and a shiny compass. "This map belonged to Captain Redbeard, a famous pirate," Grandfather said. "He buried his treasure on a small, uncharted island." Tom and Grandfather decided to follow the map. They bought a small ship and sailed for days across the blue sea. Finally, they found the island, with its sandy beaches and tall palm trees. After hours of searching, they found a chest filled with gold coins and sparkling jewels, just as the map promised.

story.title: Learning about Mary
story.text: Last Tuesday, Zoe's father bought his daughter a present from the new bookshop in the town centre. It was a story that Zoe wanted to read about a woman who sailed round the world in a very small boat. Her name was Mary Banks and she was only 22. It was a very exciting story. Sometimes the waves were really big and the wind was very strong. She saw jellyfish, whales and sharks in the sea. She was often tired but she was never frightened. When Mary came home, lots of people wanted to read about her. There was a website about Mary's trip around the world and an e-book. There was a story about her on the back page of Zoe's favourite comic too! Zoe loved reading about Mary. 'Can we buy a boat, Dad?' she asked. 'I want to sail around the world, too!' Zoe's father smiled. 'I think that's a great idea, Zoe. But first you must learn to swim!'
